OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS FILED BY DEFENDANT PERKINS, BOBBY PERKINS, ALMA JEAN

DISCOVERY 610, NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: THE MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITIONS OF NON-PARTY WITNESSES ALMA AND BOBBY PERKINS IS GRANTED. THE TIMELY OBJECTIONS THAT THE NOTICES OF DEPOSITION INVADED THE PRIVACY OF THE WITNESSES AND ARE OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF DISCOVERY ARE OVERRULED. THE SEPTEMBER 22, 2004 ORDER OF THIS COURT POSTPONING THE TRIAL DOES NOT PRECLUDE THE REQUESTED DISCOVERY. BY ORDERING THE DEPOSITIONS TO PROCEED, THE COURT TAKES NO POSITION AS TO WHETHER ANY INQUIRY MAY UNREASONABLY ANNOY, EMBARRASS, OR OPPRESS THE DEPONENT WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP SECTION 2025(N) TO JUSTIFY MOVING FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER. THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER AS TO THE SCHEDULING THE DEPOSITIONS, TAKING INTO CONSIDERATION PENDING PROCEEDINGS IN THE RELATED PROBATE ACTION. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN
